system.description:
Discipline: Qinggong
Skill: Speed TN Special
Type: Normal
Qi: 4

You focus your mind and Qi energy into one objective: get to that spot. You may move at your maximum speed, but to others it appears as though your legs are still making a walking motion. This Technique can be used in a number of situations. Each use only affords one of the below effects, which you choose, also setting the TN for your Speed roll: Meet Foe: You may use this Technique to saunter obliviously across a battlefield, but you must declare one single opponent that you are marching up to. This opponent may be farther than your normal move range, up to your normal move multiplied by your Ranks in Qinggong. Make a Speed roll TN 6. On a Success, you reach him as a Move and Skill action, and no one who you pass may make any free Attacks or attempt to interrupt your advance in any way, including the target you walk up to. On a Failure, you still make it to him, but all who you pass may react as normal. Walk on Air: You may use this Technique to walk across air as though it were solid. You must begin this Technique on solid ground, stepping off a ledge (ex. cliff face), then making a Speed roll TN 4. On a Success, you walk across air as though it were a perfectly flat, horizontal plane. You may walk a distance up to your Ranks in Qinggong + your Qi level, then multiplied by your normal move. So, a user with 2 Ranks in Qinggong and 2 Points in Speed has a normal move of 50 feet. If they were Qi level 4, 2+4=6, 6x50 = maximum range of 300 feet. On a Failure, you fall, because you cannot stand on air. Looking down at any point while using this Technique constitutes an immediate Failure, causing you to fall. This Technique was created by Reckless Storm. Cathartic: When using this Technique to Meet Foe, you may deliver a mundane Attack or Kung Fu Technique at the end of your move. When using this Technique to Walk on Air, the maximum distance of your walk increases to your Ranks in Qinggong + your Qi level, then multiplied by 200 feet. Additionally, you may treat the air you are walking on as “solid ground” for the purposes of making another immediate use of this Technique.
system.source: Wandering Heroes of Ogre Gate, Chapter 3: Kung Fu Techniques
